content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Debugging for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills setup to crush complex bugs. A high-efficiency checklist for freelancers using Antigravity to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: Stop wrestling with complex bugs manually. By integrating Claude Code with your IDE and leveraging Antigravity, you gain an autonomous debugging engine that traces stack overflows, refactors legacy spaghetti code, and executes unit tests in seconds. This setup transforms your terminal into a high-octane engineering partner for rapid delivery.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your codebase just collapsed under a cascade of silent errors. You’re staring at a terminal full of stack traces that make no sense, and the pressure to ship is mounting. We've all been there—trapped in a debugging loop that drains your billable hours and kills your momentum.

But the era of manual debugging is over. By configuring Claude Code as your primary agentic partner and pairing it with Antigravity’s structural analysis tools, you stop guessing and start fixing. This isn't just about finding errors; it's about building a robust, repeatable system that turns you into a high-velocity developer who ships flawless code on every deadline.

steps.title: Environment Audit
steps.description: Initialize your project workspace to ensure the AI has full visibility into your dependency graph and configuration files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project directory structure and identify potential bottlenecks or missing configuration files that prevent robust debugging.

steps.title: Context Loading
steps.description: Inject your architectural design patterns into the Claude Code memory cache to ensure suggestions adhere to your specific coding style.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the core architectural patterns and state management logic of this project for an AI context injection.

steps.title: Isolating the Failure
steps.description: Use trace analysis to pinpoint the exact line where the state diverges from expected behavior.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the stack trace below and correlate it with the recent code changes in the last three commits.

steps.title: Autonomous Refactoring
steps.description: Task the agent with applying a fix while ensuring no regressions are introduced.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for this module that fixes the memory leak while maintaining the existing API contract.

steps.title: Verification Loop
steps.description: Automate the test execution to confirm the resolution is permanent and scalable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of edge-case unit tests to verify the fix and prevent future regression of this specific bug.

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated .claudeignore file to prevent the agent from parsing massive minified bundles or build artifacts.
Chain your prompts: ask the agent to explain the 'why' before applying the 'how' to ensure you retain knowledge of the fix.
Use Antigravity to map dependency cycles before starting a major refactor to avoid breaking unrelated modules.
Leverage Claude Code's ability to read your local documentation files by adding a /docs directory to the context.
When facing cryptic errors, ask the agent to simulate the execution flow in its internal scratchpad before suggesting code changes.
content.next_steps: Once your debugging workflow is stable, integrate these steps into your CI/CD pipeline to automate error detection before code even hits production.
content.conversion_block: Don't let bugs dictate your delivery schedule. Arm yourself with these tools and reclaim your time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
